attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: cook and chill a slice of bread
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the task of cooking and chilling a slice of bread. The following is a detailed description based on the domain types and the visual information provided.

---

**1. Objects and Their Types**

- **Receptacles**:
  - **Microwave**: A stainless steel microwave oven with a digital display showing "OFF". It is placed on a countertop with a marble-like pattern. It is a receptacle and is openable.
  - **Fridge**: A dark-colored refrigerator with a textured surface. It has a yellow smiley face magnet and a small photo attached to its front. It is a receptacle and is openable.
  - **Sink**: Not visible in any of the images; therefore, it is not present in the current scene or not in view.
  - **Countertop**: While not explicitly defined as a receptacle in the domain, it functions as a surface holding objects. However, per the domain, only microwave, fridge, and sink are receptacles.

- **Objects**:
  - **Bread**: A single loaf of brown bread with a textured crust, sitting on the countertop. It is not yet sliced.
  - **Knife**: A kitchen knife with a dark handle and a metallic blade. It is lying flat on the countertop, near the edge, to the left of the bread.
  - **Lettuce**: Two green lettuce heads are on the countertop. They are not relevant to the current task but are present in the scene.
  - **Salt/Pepper Grinder**: A small cylindrical object with a copper-colored top and a clear body, likely a salt or pepper grinder. It is on the countertop near the lettuce.
  - **Agent**: Not visually depicted, but implied to be present and capable of interacting with objects.

---

**2. Quantities of Each Object Type**

- Microwave: 1
- Fridge: 1
- Knife: 1
- Bread: 1 loaf (un-sliced)
- Lettuce: 2 heads
- Salt/Pepper Grinder: 1
- Countertop: 1 (serves as a surface but not a domain receptacle)

---

**3. Spatial Relationships Between Objects**

- The **microwave** is on the countertop, positioned near the corner, with its display facing forward.
- The **fridge** is standing on the wooden floor, adjacent to the countertop area. Its door handle is on the right side.
- The **bread** is on the countertop, to the right of the salt/pepper grinder and near the edge of the counter.
- The **knife** is on the countertop, lying horizontally, to the left of the bread and closer to the front edge.
- The **lettuce** heads are on the countertop, one near the center and one slightly to the right, behind the salt/pepper grinder.
- The **salt/pepper grinder** is on the countertop, between the lettuce and the bread.
- The **agent** is not visible but is assumed to start at an undefined location and must navigate to objects to interact with them.

---

**4. Current State of Each Object Relevant to the Planning Task**

- **Microwave**: Currently off (display reads "OFF"). Not opened. Not holding any object.
- **Fridge**: Closed. Not opened. Not holding any object.
- **Bread**: Whole, unsliced. Not heated. Not cooled. Not cleaned. Not held by the agent.
- **Knife**: On the countertop. Not held by the agent. Not used for slicing yet.
- **Lettuce and Grinder**: Not relevant to the task; their states are not tracked in the domain for this instruction.

---

**5. Colors, Positions, and Other Visual Properties**

- **Microwave**: Stainless steel with a black control panel and red digital display. Positioned on the right side of the countertop in one view.
- **Fridge**: Dark gray or black with a textured finish. Has a yellow smiley face magnet and a small photo on the upper door. Located on the wooden floor, to the right of the countertop.
- **Bread**: Brown with a slightly glossy crust, rectangular in shape. Positioned on the countertop near the right edge.
- **Knife**: Dark handle (possibly black or dark brown) and a metallic blade. Lying flat on the countertop, oriented left-to-right.
- **Lettuce**: Bright green, leafy heads. One is closer to the center of the counter, the other is further back and to the right.
- **Salt/Pepper Grinder**: Copper-colored top, clear body with white contents visible. Small and cylindrical, near the lettuce.
- **Countertop**: Brown with black and beige marbling. Covers the top of the wooden cabinet.
- **Floor**: Light wood laminate with visible grain patterns. Extends under the fridge and countertop.

---

This scene provides all necessary objects for the task: a loaf of bread to be sliced, a knife to slice it, a microwave to heat it, and a fridge to chill it. The agent must first navigate to the knife, pick it up, slice the bread, then heat the slice in the microwave, and finally chill it in the fridge. No objects are currently in receptacles, and none have been cleaned, heated, cooled, or sliced yet.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem cook_and_chill_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        fridge1 - fridge
        knife1 - knife
        bread1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave1)
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isSliced bread1))
        (not (isHot bread1))
        (not (isCool bread1))
        (atLocation agent1 microwave1) ; Starting location can be arbitrary; we'll assume microwave for simplicity.
        (atLocation knife1 microwave1) ; Knife is on the countertop near microwave.
        (atLocation bread1 microwave1) ; Bread is on the countertop near microwave.
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isSliced bread1)
        (isHot bread1)
        (isCool bread1)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem cook_and_chill_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        fridge1 - fridge
        knife1 - knife
        bread1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave1)
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isSliced bread1))
        (not (isHot bread1))
        (not (isCool bread1))
        (atLocation agent1 microwave1) ; Starting location can be arbitrary; we'll assume microwave for simplicity.
        (atLocation knife1 microwave1) ; Knife is on the countertop near microwave.
        (atLocation bread1 microwave1) ; Bread is on the countertop near microwave.
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isSliced bread1)
        (isHot bread1)
        (isCool bread1)
    ))
)

    Instruction: cook and chill a slice of bread
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the task of cooking and chilling a slice of bread. The following is a detailed description based on the domain definitions and visual evidence:

1. **Objects and Their Types**:
   - **Receptacles**: 
     - A **microwave** is present on a countertop. It is a silver appliance with a digital display showing "OFF". It is openable and can hold objects for heating.
     - A **fridge** is visible, with a dark, textured front and a handle on the right. It has a yellow smiley face magnet and a photo attached. It is openable and can hold objects for cooling.
     - The **countertop** (not explicitly a receptacle in the domain but functions as a surface) holds several items and is made of a brown marbled material.
   - **Objects**:
     - A **loaf of bread** (brown, rectangular, with a textured crust) is on the countertop.
     - A **knife** with a dark blade and a green handle lies on the countertop.
     - Two **lettuce heads** (green, leafy) are on the countertop, but are not relevant to the current task.
     - A **pepper shaker** (copper-colored with a white body) is also on the countertop, but not relevant.
     - A **dark green plate or bowl** is partially visible on the countertop.

2. **Quantities**:
   - 1 microwave
   - 1 fridge
   - 1 loaf of bread
   - 1 knife
   - 2 lettuce heads (irrelevant)
   - 1 pepper shaker (irrelevant)
   - 1 plate/bowl (irrelevant)

3. **Spatial Relationships**:
   - The **loaf of bread** is on the countertop, near the pepper shaker and one of the lettuce heads.
   - The **knife** is on the countertop, to the left of the pepper shaker and near the edge of the counter.
   - The **microwave** is on a separate countertop section, away from the main counter with the bread and knife.
   - The **fridge** is positioned on the floor, adjacent to the countertop area, with its door facing the viewer.
   - The robot is not initially at any object’s location and must navigate to interact.

4. **Current State of Objects**:
   - The **microwave** is currently off (display reads "OFF") and not opened.
   - The **fridge** is closed (not opened).
   - The **loaf of bread** is whole (not sliced) and at room temperature (not heated or cooled).
   - The **knife** is on the countertop and not held by any agent.
   - No objects are currently held by the agent (robot).

5. **Visual Properties**:
   - The **microwave** is metallic silver with a black control panel and a glass door.
   - The **fridge** is dark gray or black with a textured surface and a prominent handle.
   - The **bread** is a light brown loaf with a slightly uneven, crusty surface.
   - The **knife** has a matte dark blade and a distinct green handle.
   - The **countertop** is brown with a marbled pattern, and the floor is light wood laminate.

To complete the task of cooking and chilling a slice of bread, the robot must first go to the countertop to pick up the knife, then slice the bread, then go to the microwave to heat the slice, and finally go to the fridge to cool it. The current state indicates that none of these actions have been performed yet.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem cook_and_chill_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        fridge - fridge
        bread - object
        knife - knife
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (opened fridge))
        (not (isSliced bread))
        (not (isHot bread))
        (not (isCool bread))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (atLocation robot microwave))
        (not (atLocation robot fridge))
        (not (atLocation robot bread))
        (not (atLocation robot knife))
        (atLocation robot countertop) ; assuming a default starting point not specified, but robot must start somewhere
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isSliced bread)
        (isHot bread)
        (isCool bread)
    ))
)
```

        microwave - microwave
        fridge - fridge
        bread - object
        knife - knife
    )

        (not (atLocation robot bread))
        (not (atLocation robot knife))
        (atLocation robot countertop) ; assuming a default starting point not specified, but robot must start somewhere
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isSliced bread)
        (isHot bread)
        (isCool bread)
    ))
)

            )

                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
